title: How Trump Failed to Secure the Strait of Hormuz in His Iran Deal
summary: President Trump signed an agreement that Iran said gave it control of the waterway — and global energy supplies. Now, Iran’s military is violently asserting authority.

title: Oil Prices Surge After Iran and U.S. Trade Strikes
summary: The renewed hostilities posed fresh risks to ships seeking to navigate the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Lindsey Graham, Republican Senator and Trump Ally, Dies From ‘Sudden Illness’
summary: He consistently pushed for the use of U.S. military power overseas. The Washington, D.C., medical examiner said he died of an aortic dissection, a tear in the body’s main artery.

title: Trump Recalls Last Conversation With Lindsey Graham Before His Death
summary: In an interview on Sunday, President Trump recounted his last conversation with Senator Lindsey Graham, hours before his death. Mr. Graham sounded “perfect,” he said.

title: Here's what we know about Lindsey Graham’s death
summary: Senator Lindsey Graham died on Saturday night, a day after returning from Ukraine and four months before he was to face re-election.

title: Japan Is Building a New Intelligence Agency With Help From the West
summary: Facing threats from Russia and China, Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi is breaking with World War II-era limits on security.

title: How Putin Turned Japan Into a Den of Spies
summary: Operating out of a Tokyo high-rise, a military intelligence unit finds the high-tech equipment that Russia needs to wage war.

title: Michael Cohen Helped Convict Trump. Now, He’s Making Nice Again.
summary: A previously unreported encounter last summer set the stage for a rapprochement between the president and his former fixer, who has so far avoided the diatribes and prosecutions that President Trump has directed at other critics.

title: Blanche Stares Down Confirmation Hurdle: Lingering G.O.P. Doubts
summary: Key Republicans on the Judiciary Committee could push for concessions from Todd Blanche, in line to be attorney general, though they did not appear in revolt.

title: Sam Neill, New Zealand Screen Actor and Leading Man, Dies at 78
summary: He appeared in more than 150 productions over a five-decade career but was perhaps best known for his star turn in the “Jurassic Park” series.

title: After Weeks of Silence, McConnell Says He Is Recovering From a Fall
summary: The Republican senator denied that he had suffered a heart attack and said he had left the hospital and moved to a physical rehabilitation center. He did not give a timetable for returning to Capitol Hill.

title: It’s the Scent of Manure to Most, but ‘the Smell of Money’ to Them
summary: The Netherlands has been trying to figure out what to do with manure it can no longer use thanks to environmental rules. Volatile fertilizer costs may help.

title: Residents of Spanish Towns Caught in Fierce Blazes Recount Harrowing Escapes
summary: Many say communication was chaotic and even nonexistent as flames tore through the area, taking 12 lives in one of the deadliest wildfires on record in Spain.

title: ‘They Don’t Need People’: The Workers Left Behind by China’s Robot Drive
summary: For out-of-work factory workers in Kunshan, a region made rich by electronics manufacturing, a park is the only place to go.

title: The World Must Not Forget About the Genocide in Sudan
summary: The longer the fighting drags on, the greater the chance that regional instability will spread.

title: The World Is Cutting Ties With America. It’s Already Costing Us.
summary: When other countries cut ties, Americans pay.
